comments.text:
MERGE GATE RED (post-S1-close): mix spec.check exited 1 with 59 verification_command_failed findings after S1 (specled_-s65.1) merged into the epic branch.

Root cause (independently confirmed by both S1's verifier and auditor, and reproduced here in the epic worktree): lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex defaults command_timeout_ms to 120000ms, but the tagged_tests verification command (`mix test --include integration <62 files>`) takes ~245-320s on this host — well over budget. .spec/config.yml sets no override. On timeout the verification port closes and every subject sharing that command gets verification_command_failed, hence 59 findings from one slow command.

Evidence this is NOT a real regression from S1:
- `mix test --include integration` run standalone: 816 tests, 0 failures (both by the auditor and just now via the full mix spec.check run — all dots, no F's, in the log before the timeout-classification errors)
- plain `mix test`: passes
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors, mix format --check-formatted, mix deps.unlock --check-unused: all pass
- Committed .spec/state.json at S1's commit (067f791) has findings: []
- S1's diff does not touch .spec/config.yml or verification timeout settings; the added tests are fast unit tests with no plausible way to add ~150-200s

This is a pre-existing condition (present on the base commit, unrelated to any child ticket's Allowed touches — S1 and S2 are both out of scope for touching verification.command_timeout_ms). Per orchestration rules, a red merge gate blocks further work regardless of root-cause analysis, so I'm pausing here rather than continuing to S2.

Options for the human:
1. Raise verification.command_timeout_ms in .spec/config.yml (the repo already ships "configurable spec verification timeout" per a recent commit) — this would need its own small ticket/change since it's out of scope for S1/S2, or a manual config edit if you consider it pre-existing repo maintenance rather than epic scope.
2. Confirm CI runs with a longer effective timeout / faster host and this is a local-sandbox-only artifact — in which case you may want to explicitly waive this gate for local orchestration and only enforce it in CI.
3. Direct me to bypass this gate for this run (I will not do so without explicit instruction, per "never bypass merge gates").

Epic paused pending your call. S1 is closed and merged; S2 (specled_-s65.2) has not been started.

SCOPE_EXPANSION: spawned specled_-s65.3 to bump verification.command_timeout_ms to 400000ms per user direction, unblocking the merge gate raised on this ticket. Does not consume a bounce. Continuing the pipeline on it before resuming S2.

MERGE GATE RESOLVED: mix spec.check now passes cleanly (findings=0, status=pass) after the verification.command_timeout_ms bump (specled_-s65.3) merged.

Note on the transient red I posted mid-run: a second mix spec.check attempt right after merging specled_-s65.3 also came back red (59 verification_command_failed, same signature as before). Root cause was my own scheduling mistake, not a regression — I had launched mix test and mix spec.check CONCURRENTLY in the same worktree, and CPU contention between the two heavy Elixir test runs pushed the tagged_tests subprocess over the 400s budget even though it fits comfortably in isolation. Re-ran mix spec.check alone (no concurrent mix test): clean pass, 0 findings, 94 verifications, exit 0. All merge gates (compile, test, format, deps.unlock, spec.check) are green on the current epic HEAD. Resuming the DAG to S2.

title: Wire realization.enabled_tiers from .spec/config.yml into the orchestrator
